content.full_text:
Office of District Attorney                Contra
                                                      Costa
Court House, Fourth Floor                             County
P.O. Box 670
Martinez, California 94553
(415) 646-4500
                                                                Gary T. Yancey
                                                                District Attorney


                                                      [SEAL]

                                          March 12, 1990


Technical Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

     RE:  Campaign Statements For Unopposed Judges

To Whom It May Concern:

My wife is a Superior Court judge for Contra Costa County.  She
recently received a "1989, Form 470, Candidate and Officeholder
Campaign Statement -- Short Form" instruction sheet as she is up
for election in June 1990.  The instruction sheet says:

     Note: Judges who will not be listed on the ballot during a
     calendar year, who have not received any contributions, and
     whose only expenditures for the year are contributions
     aggregating less than $1,000 which were made out of personal
     funds are not required to file campaign statements. (emphasis
     added)

My research, including review of Government Code section 84206,
FPPC regulation 18406, and pages 17 and 18, Section D, of the 1988
Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the
Political Reform Act, indicates that even unopposed judges who have
no controlled committee, receive no campaign funds and whose only
expense is a filing fee paid out of personal funds still must file
at least the Form 470.  In fact, the footnote on page 18 of the
above referenced Information Manual states:

     Judges who are unopposed and whose only expenditure is the
     payment of personal funds for the filing fee or statement
     of qualifications must file a campaign statement.

Since the filing deadline is March 22, 1990, I would like to know
as soon as possible whether your instruction sheet is in error or
has the law changed and my research outdated?  Also, since I
enforce the provisions of the Political Reform Act for Contra Costa
County, I need to know the current and correct requirement for
filing of campaign statements.

Your prompt response would be appreciated. If you desire, you may
call me at (415) 646-4527 if you do not have time to respond in
writing.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
James L. Sepulveda
Deputy District Attorney

1989

FORM 470
CANDIDATE AND OFFICEHOLDER CAMPAIGN STATEMENT -- SHORT FORM

• FOR USE BY CANDIDATES AND OFFICEHOLDERS WHO DO NOT HAVE A CONTROLLED COMMITTEE AND WHO DO NOT ANTICIPATE RECEIVING $1,000 OR MORE AND DO NOT ANTICIPATE SPENDING $1,000 OR MORE DURING THE ENTIRE CALENDAR YEAR.

• THE PERIOD COVERED IS ALWAYS THE CALENDAR YEAR (JANUARY 1 THROUGH DECEMBER 31).

• TO DETERMINE IF $1,000 HAS BEEN RAISED OR SPENT OR WILL BE RAISED OR SPENT, THE CANDIDATE'S PERSONAL FUNDS FOR THE FILING FEE OR STATEMENT OF QUALIFICATIONS ARE EXCLUDED. HOWEVER, SUCH PAYMENTS ARE "EXPENDITURES" AND ARE REPORTABLE IF THE CANDIDATE IS SUBSEQUENTLY REQUIRED TO FILE A CAMPAIGN STATEMENT LONG FORM (FORM 490). CONTRIBUTIONS MADE TO OTHER CANDIDATES, OFFICEHOLDERS AND COMMITTEES FROM A CANDIDATE'S OR OFFICEHOLDER'S PERSONAL FUNDS OR THE PERSONAL FUNDS OF HIS OR HER SPOUSE ARE INCLUDED FOR PURPOSES OF THE $1,000 THRESHOLD.

/ NOTE: JUDGES WHO WILL NOT BE LISTED ON THE BALLOT DURING A CALENDAR YEAR, WHO HAVE NOT RECEIVED ANY CONTRIBUTIONS, AND WHOSE ONLY EXPENDITURES FOR THE YEAR ARE CONTRIBUTIONS AGGREGATING LESS THAN $1,000 WHICH WERE MADE OUT OF PERSONAL FUNDS ARE NOT REQUIRED TO FILE CAMPAIGN STATEMENTS.

• IF THE FORM 470 IS FILED BY A CANDIDATE OR OFFICEHOLDER WITH A DECLARATION OF CANDIDACY, OR ON OR BEFORE THE FILING DEADLINE FOR THE FIRST CAMPAIGN STATEMENT REQUIRED FOR THE CURRENT CALENDAR YEAR, NO ADDITIONAL CAMPAIGN STATEMENTS NEED BE FILED FOR THAT CALENDAR YEAR SO LONG AS TOTAL RECEIPTS REMAIN LESS THAN $1,000 AND TOTAL EXPENDITURES REMAIN LESS THAN $1,000. (IN MOST CASES, THE FILING DEADLINE FOR THE FIRST CAMPAIGN STATEMENT REQUIRED TO BE FILED BY OFFICEHOLDERS AND CANDIDATES NOT BEING VOTED UPON IS JULY 31. SEE THE "INFORMATION MANUAL ON CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS OF THE POLITICAL REFORM ACT" FOR FURTHER INFORMATION.)

IMPORTANT

"COMMITTEES" MUST FILE SEMI-ANNUAL CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE STATEMENTS FOR EACH SIX-MONTH PERIOD WHETHER OR NOT THEY HAD ANY ACTIVITY DURING THE SIX-MONTH PERIOD COVERED BY THE STATEMENT.

OFFICEHOLDERS AND CANDIDATES WHO HAVE RAISED OR SPENT $1,000 OR MORE OR WHO ANTICIPATE RAISING OR SPENDING $1,000 OR MORE MUST FILE THE FORM 490, CANDIDATE AND OFFICEHOLDER CAMPAIGN STATEMENT-LONG FORM.

OFFICEHOLDERS WHOSE SALARY IS LESS THAN $100 PER MONTH AND JUDGES WHO HAVE A CONTROLLED COMMITTEE, SHOULD REFER TO THE "INFORMATION MANUAL ON CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS OF THE POLITICAL REFORM ACT" TO DETERMINE THEIR FILING OBLIGATIONS.

sections.question: Since the filing deadline is March 22, 1990, I would like to know as soon as possible whether your instruction sheet is in error or has the Law changed and my research outdated? Also, since I enforce the provisions of the Political Reform Act for Contra Costa County, I need to know the current and correct requirement for filing of campaign statements.
sections.question_synthetic: The District Attorney of Contra Costa County seeks clarification on whether an unopposed Superior Court judge with no campaign committee, no contributions, and only a personal funds filing fee payment must file a Form 470 campaign statement, noting an apparent contradiction between the Form 470 instruction sheet and the FPPC Information Manual.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document includes the Form 470 instruction sheet as a reference document. No FPPC conclusion or response is present. The letter requests urgent guidance due to a March 22, 1990 filing deadline.

Also, since I enforce the provisions of the Political Reform Act for Contra Costa County, I need to know the current and correct requirement for filing of campaign statements.

embedding.summary: The District Attorney of Contra Costa County requests urgent clarification from the FPPC regarding campaign statement filing requirements for unopposed judges, noting a discrepancy between the Form 470 instruction sheet (which appears to exempt such judges) and the FPPC Information Manual (which requires filing).
